tests.name: frame_size_1
tests.description: M2 frame with length=1 (single zero byte body)

tests.name: frame_size_65535
tests.description: M2 frame claiming length=65535 with short body

tests.name: size_mismatch_100_10
tests.description: Frame says 100 bytes but only 10 sent

tests.name: three_frames_backtoback
tests.description: Three valid M2 frames sent consecutively

tests.name: 20_frames_rapid
tests.description: 20 valid M2 frames in one send

tests.name: fragmented_frame
tests.description: Send first half of frame, wait 1s, send rest

tests.name: length_only_no_body
tests.description: Send only 2-byte length prefix (100) with no body

tests.name: large_frame_60KB
tests.description: M2 frame with 60KB of random body

tests.name: little_endian_length
tests.description: Frame with little-endian length (wrong byte order)

tests.name: all_zeros_20
tests.description: 20 zero bytes (could be interpreted as length=0 + more zeros)

tests.name: http_on_winbox
tests.description: HTTP GET request to Winbox port (protocol confusion)

tests.name: api_on_winbox
tests.description: RouterOS API /login sentence to Winbox port

tests.name: truncated_kv_start
tests.description: Frame body is just a type byte (0x08) with no key_id or value

tests.name: nested_frame
tests.description: M2 frame wrapping another complete M2 frame

tests.name: zero_length_frame_only
tests.description: Frame with length=0 and no body bytes

tests.name: abrupt_close_partial
tests.description: Send partial frame then close connection
